details: prelu_sfpu not found in unary op_utils - checking if deep-nuked. Found TT_THROW in binary_composite_op.cpp. Checking key notes and docs for implementation details.
Read unary_op_utils.cpp - DROPOUT enum exists in types but not in dispatch switch cases (nuked codebase)

HARDTANH is listed in is_parametrized_type() but missing from get_op_init_and_func_parameterized and get_op_init_and_func_default switches - this is a deeply nuked codebase. Need to search for hardtanh SFPU implementation files.

prelu_sfpu has been deep-nuked from this repo. The API header prelu.h, the SFPU ckernel, and the unary_op_utils dispatch code are all removed. Must reconstruct from docs, key_notes, reference patterns in existing similar ops (leaky_relu), and the unary_eltwise_sfpu_list.md catalog.

LEAKY_RELU has been fully nuked from this branch (Phase 2 of deep nuke). The enum value, registration, dispatch, and all SFPU primitives are removed. This is an evaluation environment for rrelu. The original implementation used _calculate_lrelu_ in ckernel_sfpu_relu.h.
Nuked codebase - API headers for dropout are removed. Tracing from available: compute kernel (.cpp), ckernel_sfpu_dropout.h, and LLK layer. Need to find llk_math layer.

DEEP_NUKE_MANIFEST confirms hardtanh: dispatch removed, compute API deleted, Metal ckernel deleted, Metal LLK deleted. BUT ckernel_sfpu_hardtanh.h STILL EXISTS in tt_llk (wh+bh). Can analyze the core SFPU implementation.

note: Operation deep-nuked. Dispatch reconstructed from docs/sfpu_operations/unary_eltwise_sfpu_list.md, Doxyfile, and prelu_tile.rst. API signature: prelu_tile(uint32_t idst, uint32_t param0) and prelu_tile_init().

details: Traced the full SFPU abstraction layers for this nuked repo. LEAKY_RELU enum, SfpuType, compute API, LLK dispatch, and ckernel implementation are all removed. Only surviving ops are frac, swish, atanh, sinh. The original implementation was _calculate_lrelu_ in ckernel_sfpu_relu.h. Pattern analysis from threshold and sign shows the leaky_relu kernel used v_if(x < 0) { dst = x * slope } v_endif conditional branching.
Has own program factory in experimental/dropout/, not using UnaryProgramFactory. Compute kernel: dropout_kernel.cpp, API call: dropout_tile(0, int_probability, int_scale_factor)

note: LEAKY_RELU has been fully nuked from this branch. Enum, registration, dispatch, compute API, LLK, and ckernel all removed. Analysis reconstructs the original architecture from surviving patterns and nuke manifest.

key_findings: All three architecture variants are gutted (pragma once only). Original implementation _calculate_lrelu_ used SFPI abstractions (vFloat, v_if, dst_reg). Pattern reconstructed from nuke manifest + surviving threshold/sign kernels.

Uses s2vFloat16b for param loading, vFloat arithmetic (add), v_if/v_endif for clamping, 3 params in FP16_B: p0=-neg_threshold, p1=-(pos_threshold-neg_threshold), p2=-pos_threshold. WH and BH ckernel_sfpu_hardtanh.h are identical.
uses SFPLOADI/SFPLOAD/SFPMUL/SFPMOV(PRNG)/SFPSETSGN/SFPIADD/SFPMOV/SFPENCC/SFPSTORE with CC manipulation for conditional zeroing

prelu_sfpu kernel was deep-nuked. Reconstructed from: (1) formula max(0,x)+weight*min(0,x), (2) prelu_tile.rst API signature: prelu_tile(uint32_t idst, uint32_t param0) + prelu_tile_init(), (3) SFPU_OP_PRELU_INCLUDE guard, (4) one weight param passed as uint32_t, (5) surviving similar ops (threshold, clamp, hardtanh) all use v_if/v_endif SFPI style

addr_mod: ADDR_MOD_7 (.dest.incr=0, standard for non-special unary ops)
note: Instruction analysis reconstructed from surviving pattern ops (threshold, sign) since original leaky_relu kernel was nuked.

key_findings: Uses SFPI abstractions (vFloat, dst_reg, v_if/v_endif). Simple conditional: if in <= threshold, replace with value. WH and BH core kernels identical. Params dispatch uses _llk_math_eltwise_unary_sfpu_params_ with VectorMode::RC. ADDR_MOD_7 with all-zero increments on both WH and BH.

instructions_decoded: SFPMAD (via vFloat multiplication weight*in and addition)
addr_mod: ADDR_MOD_7 (dest.incr=0, standard for most unary SFPU ops)
